target: kafka namespace, pod kspfail-single-0 (Strimzi single-broker KRaft Kafka)
corroborating_evidence: kubectl events: Killing -> Scheduled -> Created -> Pulled(cached) -> Started -> 3x Unhealthy(readiness probe) -> Ready
Prometheus /api/v1/targets: kafka-broker-jmx target health unknown from 14:54:09Z to 14:55:03Z, up from 14:55:06Z
Prometheus query_range up{job="kafka-broker-jmx"}: new instance label (10.244.0.8:9404) first appears ~70s post-injection
methodological_note: up{} for the OLD instance (10.244.0.6) does not drop to 0 within the observed window because Kubernetes pod-based service discovery drops the dead target from the active list entirely rather than continuing to scrape-and-fail it; PromQL's 5-minute staleness lookback then forward-fills the last real sample (1) for the old series until it formally goes stale. The reliable, K8s-native ground-truth signal for a pod-recreate fault is therefore NOT a clean up==0 dip but (a) target instance-label churn (old IP disappears, new IP appears) and (b) the old series going absent only after the ~5 min staleness window elapses. This is a documented finding for the labeling methodology (Section 6.3 of research_context.md): sliding-window feature construction for this fault class must account for instance-identity churn on pod recreation, not assume a stable single time series per broker across a restart.
